taxonomy: Pilasia filifolia is classified as a species of liverwort, specifically within the family Calypogeiaceae. The genus Pilasia belongs to the class Marchantiopsida and the division Marchantiophyta, which consists of liverworts. Liverworts are non-vascular plants that belong to the kingdom Plantae, but are distinct from flowering plants and conifers. They are often found in damp, shaded environments and play a crucial role in decomposing organic matter. The classification of Pilasia filifolia highlights its unique characteristics and its position within the plant kingdom.
